message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To assess left atrial appendage (LAA) thrombus detection using a novel cardiac computed tomography (CT) protocol specifically prior to direct current cardioversion (DCCV).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) is the gold standard in evaluating the LAA prior to DCCV for atrial fibrillation (AF) or flutter (AFL). The COVID-19 pandemic has restricted availability of TEE and anesthesia support.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Prospective cohort of consecutive patients with uncontrolled AF/AFL referred for DCCV from March 2020 to March 2021 were enrolled. CT evaluation of LAA was performed with an initial arterial and subsequent 30-second delayed acquisition to exclude thrombus prior to DCCV. Primary end points were to assess safety and outcomes.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    A total of 161 patients were included; mean age 69.8 ± 11.1 years; mean CHA
                    <jats:sub>2</jats:sub>
                    DS
                    <jats:sub>2</jats:sub>
                    -VASc 3.4 ± 1.7; and HAS-BLED 1.8 ± 0.9. Median follow-up 175 (105-267) days with zero cardiac-related deaths, and no episodes of TIA or embolic stroke. There was no statistically significant change in mean glomerular filtration rate (GFR) in the study population between the pre- and post-GFR measurements (73.9 ± 21.0 vs 72.7 ± 20.3; p=0.104). Overall mean total dose length product (DLP) was 1042.8 ± 447.5 mGy*cm. Modifying the CT protocol to a narrower 8-cm axial coverage had a statistically significant decrease in total DLP (from 1130.6 ± 464.1 mGy*cm to 802.1 ± 286.4 mGy*cm; P<0.0001).
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Cardiac CT is both a safe and feasible alternative imaging to TEE for patients prior to DCCV.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Perspectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Competency in Medical Knowledge</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Cardiac CT is a powerful tool for the evaluation of left atrial appendage and detection of thrombus prior to direct current cardioversion.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Translational Outlook</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Our protocol was implemented with the restrictions during COVID-19 in mind, yet CT can be a viable tool beyond the pandemic; future randomized clinical trials can bridge the gap between CT and TEE in the setting of cardioversion and help elucidate its safety profile further.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                </jats:sec>
title: CT-Guided Direct Current Cardioversion for Atrial Arrhythmias During the COVID-19 Pandemic
